name: Malware Attribute Enumeration and Characterization
description: MAEC was a MITRE-led community effort to standardize the encoding and communication of high-fidelity information about malware (behaviors, capabilities, artifacts, relationships). The MAEC schema (XML, later JSON) supported automated malware analysis sharing. The project went inactive after MITRE shifted focus to ATT&CK and STIX/TAXII (which absorbed most malware-characterization use cases). Last formal MAEC release was 5.0 (2017); the effort is effectively superseded by ATT&CK Software entries and STIX malware extensions.
relationToCloudSecurity: MAEC is no longer actively maintained. For current malware-characterization work — including cloud-targeting malware — use MITRE ATT&CK's Software catalog and STIX 2.1 malware objects.
relationToAISecurity: MAEC does not address AI/ML-targeting malware. ATT&CK and ATLAS provide AI-relevant adversary technique coverage.

license.notes: MAEC was distributed under MITRE's community-friendly terms. The project repository is archived; use ATT&CK or STIX for current work.

lifecycle.supersededBy: MITRE ATT&CK Software catalog and STIX 2.1 malware extensions
lifecycle.notes: Retained in the registry for historical reference and to resolve older citations. New malware-characterization work should use ATT&CK + STIX 2.1.

current_extraction.notes: README-only stub. No structured extraction is planned because the underlying project is inactive.

extraction_history.reason: README only — placeholder for namespace coverage

notes: Inactive MITRE project. Listed for namespace completeness; redirect any active work to MITRE ATT&CK.

desired_end_state.notes: Inactive MITRE project but namespace retained for completeness. Upstream (maecproject.github.io) is the canonical reference if anyone needs it.
